mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative 60-day return in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as markets price in higher near-term corporate output.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods where industrial production growth accelerates, or the signal fails to predict returns during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.4% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon through improved consumer spending forecasts.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the correlation between the interaction term and forward returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly expands corporate revenue baselines, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions catch up to macroeconomic output data.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds the long-term trend, or the signal shows zero predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden supply-side contraction that disrupts production chains and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where industrial production falls below the 0.5% threshold.
